title: Database Transactions & Optimistic Locking
input_format: Transaction operations (begin, read, write, commit, rollback) with concurrent execution scenarios
output_format: Transaction results, conflict detection, and consistency guarantees
example_input: T1: begin(); read(account_A); write(account_A, balance - 100); commit(); T2: begin(); read(account_A); write(account_A, balance - 50); commit();
example_output: T1: committed. T2: conflict detected, retry with updated balance.
constraints: Must implement ACID properties. Must support optimistic concurrency with version numbers. Must detect and handle write conflicts. Must support rollback on failure.
function_signature: class TransactionManager { begin(): Transaction; commit(tx: Transaction): Promise<CommitResult>; rollback(tx: Transaction): void; withTransaction<T>(fn: (tx: Transaction) => Promise<T>): Promise<T>; }
